title: Level 242
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 242: "Barrens"}}{{CSS|content=/* Page */ .page { border: solid 2px #ffffff; margin-top: 12px; position: relative; border-radius: 5px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 20px 0 #ffffff; --theme-link-color: #8585ff; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark {--theme-page-background-color: rgba(40,40,40,0.90); --theme-border-color: #ffffff;} .theme-fandomdesktop-light {--theme-page-background-color: rgba(190,190,190,100); --theme-border-color: #ffffff;} }}{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.cdnfonts.com/css/dec-terminal-modern'); html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'DEC Terminal Modern' }}{{Blurbackground|file=Can-brutalism-be-liminal-v0-J2coP49uXlH_2sX7CwvEVn1JD6fEnAWF1M2rjmN1ULA.webp}}{{Alt Class|no=242|ext=2|env=3|ent=1}} {{Audio|file=Dusty Wind.mp3|loop=yes|secrettext=wind go woosh|round=yes|align=middle|volume=1}}{{Blurimage|file=Can-brutalism-be-liminal-v0-J2coP49uXlH_2sX7CwvEVn1JD6fEnAWF1M2rjmN1ULA.webp|caption=A photograph of the level taken by 2 [[M.E.G.]]. personnel.}} == Description == '''Level 242''' is a barren complex spanning an estimated three hundred million square miles, consisting mostly of concrete buildings and terraces reminiscent of the brutalist or industrial structures that can be seen within the [[The Frontrooms]]. The buildings within the level seem to serve no inherent purpose, however, they can seldom provide shelter to the level's harsh daytime conditions. These structures can have a wide range of architectural features, ranging from large concrete arches, flat dusty roofs, and on rare occasions, basements. These basements will usually be fairly basic and devoid of defining features, save or the infrequent light fixture or lead pipe, but they can provide sufficient shelter and shade to wanderers who have entered the level. The concrete that these deformed structures are constructed of has exhibited extreme weather resistance, along with a self-restoration property similar to that of Roman concrete. Its unique properties have caught the interest of [[Architect Industries]], and teams have been sent to extract it for use in levels with harsh climate. While it is still generally unknown what compounds give the concrete its durability, chemists have analyzed trace amounts of tricalcium silicate and tungsten, along with other unidentified compounds. The level's perpetual, unbroken, cloudless sky and arid temperatures can lead to heat exhaustion in wanderers, or when left exposed for an extended period, heat stroke. It is recommended to bring large quantities of water or any other hydrating fluid if you or other persons intend to stay in the level for an extended period of time, as there are no naturally occurring sources of water that appear on the surface of the level, and no rainfall has ever been recorded up to this date. The day and night cycle of '''Level 242''' is similar to ours, being 24 hours, however the level's balance on aforementioned hours is grossly different. '''Level 242's''' night and day cycles consist of 20 hours of daytime and 4 hours of nighttime, and with the 20 hours of constant heat beating down on the dry surface of the level, inert dust can become superheated when hit by a rare instance of wind, creating a deadly weather phenomenon, dust storms. == Dust storms == {{Blurimage|caption=A picture of the level before a dust storm. (Photographer unknown)|file=Answering-the-brutalist-call-v0-cpigc9wwknbe1.webp|align=right}} Dust storms are a somewhat rare occurrence in this level, however, being unprepared when said storm hits can lead to severe injury or death. Dust storms in this level can cause third-degree burns due to the fact that the swirling debris inside these storms can reach very high temperatures and typically have wind speeds of around 120 kph. It is recommended that, when such an event occurs wanderers must:
{{User:I am a kangaroo/thisexistsfornoreasonbesidestofixsomethingforapage}} A common yet unreliable method of predetermining when a storm will make landfall is by checking to see if the hair on your body is charged with static electricity. It is unknown why air particles become charged by static electricity, with theories suggesting the underlying cause is friction, albeit no current research has come to a certain conclusion. ==The Underground== Seldom, an opening on the surface area opens up to reveal an underlying subterranea, the architecture that makes up the underground can vary from tight interconnected rooms to massive chambers supported by gargantuan concrete pillars. This space is proved to not be a sub-level thanks to [[Phenomenon 13|sufficient research.]] {{Blurimage|caption=An image of The Underground, showcasing a small room relative to the rest of the Underground.|file=Brutalist basement.jpg}} The chambers are thought to encompass all of the level's underground, providing shelter from dust storms, and creating an environment suitable for human occupation. There is also an extreme increase of the Slab populace in the basement, primarily thought to on account of humidity. The chambers are also one of the only places to have naturally occuring water, as condensation will create water droplets on the walls and pillars, and on some occasions puddles will form. ==Entities== Although the environment might seem borderline inhospitable, life still exists here and has taken on forms to match their hostile environment. === Slabs: === {{Blurimage|file=Slab.png|caption=A composite sketch of a slab}} ---- ==== Description & Behavior ==== ---- Slabs, or more scientifically ''Plana Tabulus'' are the only entities that inhabit '''Level 242'''. They appear as large, lumpy creatures that possess a hard, concrete shell and humanoid teeth whom camouflage themselves within concrete. They generally do not pose a threat, as they only scavenge for carcasses, which may explain their grotesque appearance. Their shell is also useful in protecting the creature from the dust storms within the level and keeps other slabs from consuming them. Slabs range in different diameters, most commonly being 1.5m long and 1.2m across, however, some individuals have reported seeing slabs that reached sizes of 7ft long and 9ft across. ---- ==== Biology & Taxonomy ==== ---- Slabs can also serve as an emergency food source, that being said, its flesh has been recorded to taste predominantly like iron and rotten eggs. Even with its flavor, the flesh is only safe to eat after being thoroughly cooked. Relevantly, a special water storage sac inside the entity's lower half can quench thirst. The knowledge about Slabs' taxa and general taxonomical information remain relatively unknown, as no entities have been identified to be taxonomically related. Theories suggest they may be distantly related to [[Entity 100|''Mensinficiatis desiderium'']] which renders them in the family Mensificiatidae and in the kingdom Venatrexima.
{{Blurimage|file=Slab sightingnew.png|caption=A sighting of a Slab. Photographed by [[M.E.G]] personnel, and highlighted for viewer convenience.|align=center}}
== Colonies and outposts == '''M.E.G. OUTPOST SUNDIAL:''' Outpost Sundial is an encampment created by the M.E.G. for general storage and examination of level 242's concrete. It also functions as a training camp for M.E.G. personel to simulate desert levels in a controlled environment, as the level is relatively stable enough for long periods of time. Outpost sundial also provides extra income to the main MEG branch by selling level 242's concrete to Architect Industries. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === Entering through a door with dust coming from beneath it in [[Level 38]] Descending into a sandstone pit in [[Level 46]] Noclipping into a tree in [[Level 110]] Finding a brutalist building in [[Level 241]] can lead here Entering some doors in [[Level 2]] has a chance to be an entrance here === Exits === Finding a large concrete structure with a metal door leads you into [[Level 11]] Finding a steel wall and noclipping into it will send you to [[Level 828]] Noclipping into a dried tree stump will bring you to [[Level 110]] Finding slums in the distance eventually transitions the level to [[Level 243]]; Getting lost at night can lead you to [[Level 135]]; Finding a wind turbine leads to [[Level 241]]; Entering a massive complex in the basement leads to [[Level 269]]; Finding a metallic structure leads to [[Level 411]] {{Redirect|Prev=241|Next=243}}
